title: Solving Optimization Problems with SCIP
text: scip package provides R interface SCIP (Solving Constraint Integer Programs), one fastest non-commercial solvers mixed-integer programming (MIP) mixed-integer nonlinear programming (MINLP). SCIP supports linear, quadratic, SOS, indicator constraints continuous, binary, integer variables. vignette demonstrates package examples adapted SCIP documentation example suite (Copyright 2002–2026 Zuse Institute Berlin, Apache-2.0 license). package offers two interfaces: One-shot interface (scip_solve): pass constraint matrix get solution back one call. Model-building interface (scip_model, scip_add_var, etc.): build model incrementally, useful complex formulations.

title: Solving Optimization Problems with SCIP
text: company produces two products. unit Product 1 yields $5 profit unit Product 2 yields $4 profit. Production limited labour (6 hours available) materials (8 kg available). Product 1 requires 1 hour 2 kg per unit; Product 2 requires 2 hours 1 kg per unit. max5x1+4x2\max\; 5x_1 + 4x_2 subject : x1+2x2≤6(labour)x_1 + 2x_2 \le 6 \quad\text{(labour)}2x1+x2≤8(materials)2x_1 + x_2 \le 8 \quad\text{(materials)}x1,x2≥0x_1, x_2 \ge 0
code: A <- matrix(c(1, 2,               2, 1), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE) b <- c(6, 8) ## scip_solve minimizes, so negate the objective for maximization res <- scip_solve(obj = c(-5, -4), A = A, b = b,                   sense = c("<=", "<="),                   control = list(verbose = FALSE)) res$status #> [1] "optimal" -res$objval  # negate back to get the maximized profit #> [1] 22 res$x #> [1] 3.333333 1.333333

title: Solving Optimization Problems with SCIP
text: Adapted SCIP’s solveknapsackexactly.c unit test (Marc Pfetsch, Gregor Hendel, Zuse Institute Berlin). hiker can carry 13 kg. Six items available weights 7, 2, 7, 5, 1, 3 kg, equal value. items hiker pack carry many possible? max∑=16xi\max\; \sum_{=1}^{6} x_i subject : 7x1+2x2+7x3+5x4+x5+3x6≤137x_1 + 2x_2 + 7x_3 + 5x_4 + x_5 + 3x_6 \le 13xi∈{0,1}x_i \\{0,1\}
code: A <- matrix(c(7, 2, 7, 5, 1, 3), nrow = 1) res <- scip_solve(obj = c(-1, -1, -1, -1, -1, -1),                   A = A, b = 13, sense = "<=",                   vtype = "B",                   control = list(verbose = FALSE)) res$status #> [1] "optimal" items_packed <- which(res$x == 1) items_packed #> [1] 1 2 5 6 total_weight <- sum(c(7, 2, 7, 5, 1, 3)[items_packed]) total_weight #> [1] 13

title: Solving Optimization Problems with SCIP
text: Adapted SCIP’s Queens example (examples/Queens/src/queens.cpp, Cornelius Schwarz, University Bayreuth). Place nn queens n×nn \times n chessboard two queens attack . classic combinatorial problem naturally modelled binary integer program. Let xi,j∈{0,1}x_{,j} \\{0,1\} indicate whether queen placed row ii, column jj. constraints : Rows: exactly one queen per row, ∑jxi,j=1\sum_j x_{,j} = 1 Columns: exactly one queen per column, ∑ixi,j=1\sum_i x_{,j} = 1 Diagonals: one queen per diagonal, ∑(,j)∈Dxi,j≤1\sum_{(,j)\D} x_{,j} \le 1 Visualize board (. = empty, Q = queen):
code: solve_nqueens <- function(n) {     m <- scip_model(paste0(n, "-queens"))     scip_set_objective_sense(m, "maximize")      ## Create n*n binary variables x[i,j] with objective 1     ## Variable (i,j) is stored at index (i-1)*n + j     idx <- function(i, j) (i - 1L) * n + j     scip_add_vars(m, obj = rep(1, n * n), lb = 0, ub = 1, vtype = "B",                   names = sprintf("x%d_%d", rep(1:n, each = n), rep(1:n, n)))      ## Row constraints: exactly one queen per row     for (i in 1:n) {         scip_add_linear_cons(m, vars = idx(i, 1:n), coefs = rep(1, n),                              lhs = 1, rhs = 1, name = sprintf("row_%d", i))     }      ## Column constraints: exactly one queen per column     for (j in 1:n) {         scip_add_linear_cons(m, vars = idx(1:n, j), coefs = rep(1, n),                              lhs = 1, rhs = 1, name = sprintf("col_%d", j))     }      ## Diagonal constraints: at most one queen per diagonal     ## Down-right diagonals     for (d in (-(n - 2)):(n - 2)) {         rows <- max(1, 1 + d):min(n, n + d)         cols <- rows - d         if (length(rows) >= 2) {             scip_add_linear_cons(m, vars = idx(rows, cols),                                  coefs = rep(1, length(rows)),                                  rhs = 1, name = sprintf("diag_down_%d", d))         }     }     ## Down-left diagonals (anti-diagonals)     for (s in 3:(2 * n - 1)) {         rows <- max(1, s - n):min(n, s - 1)         cols <- s - rows         if (length(rows) >= 2) {             scip_add_linear_cons(m, vars = idx(rows, cols),                                  coefs = rep(1, length(rows)),                                  rhs = 1, name = sprintf("diag_up_%d", s))         }     }      scip_optimize(m)     sol <- scip_get_solution(m)      ## Extract queen positions     x_mat <- matrix(round(sol$x), nrow = n, byrow = TRUE)     positions <- which(x_mat == 1, arr.ind = TRUE)     colnames(positions) <- c("row", "col")     list(status = scip_get_status(m),          n_queens = as.integer(sol$objval),          positions = positions[order(positions[, "row"]), ],          board = x_mat) } result <- solve_nqueens(8) result$status #> [1] "optimal" result$n_queens #> [1] 8 result$positions #>      row col #> [1,]   1   4 #> [2,]   2   6 #> [3,]   3   1 #> [4,]   4   5 #> [5,]   5   2 #> [6,]   6   8 #> [7,]   7   3 #> [8,]   8   7 board_str <- apply(result$board, 1, function(row) {     paste(ifelse(row == 1, "Q", "."), collapse = " ") }) cat(board_str, sep = "\n") #> . . . Q . . . . #> . . . . . Q . . #> Q . . . . . . . #> . . . . Q . . . #> . Q . . . . . . #> . . . . . . . Q #> . . Q . . . . . #> . . . . . . Q .

title: Solving Optimization Problems with SCIP
text: Adapted SCIP’s CallableLibrary example (examples/CallableLibrary/src/circlepacking.c, Jose Salmeron Stefan Vigerske, Zuse Institute Berlin). Pack nn circles given radii rectangle minimum area. circle ii radius rir_i, find center coordinates (xi,yi)(x_i, y_i). rectangle width WW height HH. Constraints: Circles stay within rectangle: ri≤xi≤W−rir_i \le x_i \le W - r_i ri≤yi≤H−rir_i \le y_i \le H - r_i Circles overlap: (xi−xj)2+(yi−yj)2≥(ri+rj)2(x_i - x_j)^2 + (y_i - y_j)^2 \ge (r_i + r_j)^2 <ji < j Objective: Minimize W×HW \times H (area). Since bilinear objectives harder handle, use simpler approach: minimize W+HW + H proxy fix one dimension. minimize width given fixed height, packing 3 circles.
code:
radii <- c(1.0, 1.5, 0.8) n <- length(radii) H <- 5.0  # fixed height  m <- scip_model("circlepacking")  ## Variables: x_i, y_i for each circle, plus W x_idx <- integer(n) y_idx <- integer(n) for (i in seq_len(n)) {     x_idx[i] <- scip_add_var(m, obj = 0, lb = radii[i], ub = 100,                               name = sprintf("x%d", i))     y_idx[i] <- scip_add_var(m, obj = 0, lb = radii[i], ub = H - radii[i],                               name = sprintf("y%d", i)) } w_idx <- scip_add_var(m, obj = 1, lb = 0, ub = 100, name = "W")  ## x_i <= W - r_i  =>  x_i - W <= -r_i for (i in seq_len(n)) {     scip_add_linear_cons(m, vars = c(x_idx[i], w_idx), coefs = c(1, -1),                          rhs = -radii[i],                          name = sprintf("fit_x_%d", i)) }  ## Non-overlap: (x_i - x_j)^2 + (y_i - y_j)^2 >= (r_i + r_j)^2 ## Expand: x_i^2 - 2*x_i*x_j + x_j^2 + y_i^2 - 2*y_i*y_j + y_j^2 >= (r_i+r_j)^2 for (i in 1:(n - 1)) {     for (j in (i + 1):n) {         min_dist_sq <- (radii[i] + radii[j])^2         scip_add_quadratic_cons(m,             quadvars1 = c(x_idx[i], x_idx[i], x_idx[j], y_idx[i], y_idx[i], y_idx[j]),             quadvars2 = c(x_idx[i], x_idx[j], x_idx[j], y_idx[i], y_idx[j], y_idx[j]),             quadcoefs = c(1, -2, 1, 1, -2, 1),             lhs = min_dist_sq,             name = sprintf("nooverlap_%d_%d", i, j))     } }  scip_optimize(m) cat("Status:", scip_get_status(m), "
") #> Status: optimal sol <- scip_get_solution(m) W_opt <- sol$x[w_idx] cat(sprintf("Minimum width: %.3f (height fixed at %.1f)
", W_opt, H)) #> Minimum width: 3.515 (height fixed at 5.0) for (i in seq_len(n)) {     cat(sprintf("  Circle %d (r=%.1f): center = (%.3f, %.3f)
",                 i, radii[i], sol$x[x_idx[i]], sol$x[y_idx[i]])) } #>   Circle 1 (r=1.0): center = (1.000, 4.000) #>   Circle 2 (r=1.5): center = (1.500, 1.500) #>   Circle 3 (r=0.8): center = (2.715, 3.453)

title: Solving Optimization Problems with SCIP
text: Adapted SCIP’s SCFLP example (examples/SCFLP/doc/xternal_scflp.c, Zuse Institute Berlin). company must decide pp potential warehouse locations open. warehouse ii fixed opening cost fif_i capacity kik_i. qq customers jj demand djd_j per-unit shipping cost cijc_{ij} warehouse ii. Minimize total cost. min∑ifiyi+∑,jcijxij\min\; \sum_i f_i y_i + \sum_{,j} c_{ij} x_{ij} subject : ∑ixij≥dj∀j(demand)\sum_i x_{ij} \ge d_j \quad\forall j \quad\text{(demand)}∑jxij≤kiyi∀(capacity)\sum_j x_{ij} \le k_i\, y_i \quad\forall \quad\text{(capacity)}yi∈{0,1},xij≥0y_i \\{0,1\},\; x_{ij} \ge 0
code:
## Problem data p <- 3  # warehouses q <- 4  # customers fixed_cost <- c(100, 150, 120) capacity <- c(50, 60, 40) demand <- c(20, 25, 15, 30) ## Shipping cost matrix (warehouses x customers) ship_cost <- matrix(c(     4, 8, 5, 6,     6, 3, 7, 4,     5, 5, 4, 8 ), nrow = p, byrow = TRUE)  m <- scip_model("facility_location")  ## Binary variables y_i: open warehouse i? y_idx <- integer(p) for (i in 1:p) {     y_idx[i] <- scip_add_var(m, obj = fixed_cost[i], vtype = "B",                               name = sprintf("y%d", i)) }  ## Continuous variables x_ij: amount shipped from i to j x_idx <- matrix(0L, nrow = p, ncol = q) for (i in 1:p) {     for (j in 1:q) {         x_idx[i, j] <- scip_add_var(m, obj = ship_cost[i, j],                                      lb = 0, ub = max(capacity),                                      name = sprintf("x%d_%d", i, j))     } }  ## Demand constraints: sum_i x_ij >= d_j for (j in 1:q) {     scip_add_linear_cons(m, vars = x_idx[, j], coefs = rep(1, p),                          lhs = demand[j],                          name = sprintf("demand_%d", j)) }  ## Capacity constraints: sum_j x_ij - k_i * y_i <= 0 for (i in 1:p) {     scip_add_linear_cons(m,                          vars = c(x_idx[i, ], y_idx[i]),                          coefs = c(rep(1, q), -capacity[i]),                          rhs = 0,                          name = sprintf("capacity_%d", i)) }  scip_optimize(m) sol <- scip_get_solution(m) cat("Status:", scip_get_status(m), "
") #> Status: optimal cat("Total cost:", sol$objval, "
") #> Total cost: 600  open_warehouses <- which(round(sol$x[y_idx]) == 1) cat("Open warehouses:", open_warehouses, "
") #> Open warehouses: 1 2  shipments <- matrix(sol$x[x_idx], nrow = p) cat("
Shipment plan (rows=warehouses, cols=customers):
") #>  #> Shipment plan (rows=warehouses, cols=customers): rownames(shipments) <- paste0("W", 1:p) colnames(shipments) <- paste0("C", 1:q) print(round(shipments, 1)) #>    C1 C2 C3 C4 #> W1 20  0 15  0 #> W2  0 25  0 30 #> W3  0  0  0  0

title: Solving Optimization Problems with SCIP
text: Adapted SCIP’s indicator test instances (check/instances/Indicator/, Zuse Institute Berlin). Indicator constraints model logical implications: “binary variable 1, linear constraint must hold.” widely used scheduling, network design, disjunctive programming. model simple production problem setup costs: machine must “turned ” (zi=1z_i = 1) can produce. Turning machine costs $50. unit produced machine ii earns revenue rir_i. Machine ii can produce uiu_i units, turned . max∑(rixi−50zi)\max\; \sum_i (r_i x_i - 50 z_i) subject : zi=1⟹xi≤ui(capacity )z_i = 1 \implies x_i \le u_i \quad \text{(capacity )}zi=0⟹xi=0(nothing )z_i = 0 \implies x_i = 0 \quad \text{(nothing )}xi≥0,zi∈{0,1}x_i \ge 0,\; z_i \\{0,1\} model zi=0⟹xi≤0z_i = 0 \implies x_i \le 0 indicator constraint negated variable. Equivalently, add big-M constraint xi≤uizix_i \le u_i z_i.
code:
revenue <- c(12, 8, 15) max_prod <- c(10, 20, 8) setup_cost <- 50  m <- scip_model("setup_production") scip_set_objective_sense(m, "maximize")  z_idx <- integer(3) x_idx <- integer(3) for (i in 1:3) {     z_idx[i] <- scip_add_var(m, obj = -setup_cost, vtype = "B",                               name = sprintf("z%d", i))     x_idx[i] <- scip_add_var(m, obj = revenue[i], lb = 0, ub = max_prod[i],                               name = sprintf("x%d", i))     ## x_i <= max_prod[i] * z_i  =>  x_i - max_prod[i] * z_i <= 0     scip_add_linear_cons(m, vars = c(x_idx[i], z_idx[i]),                          coefs = c(1, -max_prod[i]),                          rhs = 0,                          name = sprintf("link_%d", i)) }  scip_optimize(m) sol <- scip_get_solution(m) cat("Status:", scip_get_status(m), "
") #> Status: optimal cat("Profit:", sol$objval, "
") #> Profit: 250 for (i in 1:3) {     on <- round(sol$x[z_idx[i]])     prod <- sol$x[x_idx[i]]     cat(sprintf("  Machine %d: %s, produce %.0f units
",                 i, if (on) "ON" else "OFF", prod)) } #>   Machine 1: ON, produce 10 units #>   Machine 2: ON, produce 20 units #>   Machine 3: ON, produce 8 units

title: Solving Optimization Problems with SCIP
text: Use scip_control() one-shot interface, scip_set_param() model-building interface, tune solver behavior. Common SCIP parameters: See SCIP parameter documentation complete list.
code: ## One-shot: time limit and gap tolerance ctrl <- scip_control(verbose = FALSE, time_limit = 60, gap_limit = 0.01)  ## Model-building: set SCIP parameters directly m <- scip_model("tuning_example") scip_set_param(m, "display/verblevel", 0L)   # suppress output scip_set_param(m, "limits/time", 60.0)        # 60-second time limit scip_set_param(m, "limits/gap", 0.01)         # 1% optimality gap

what: Interface to the SCIP Optimization Suite
title: Interface to the SCIP Optimization Suite
text: R interface SCIP Optimization Suite (2025, doi:10.48550/arXiv.2411.14927). SCIP one fastest non-commercial solvers mixed integer programming (MIP) mixed integer nonlinear programming (MINLP). also framework constraint integer programming branch-cut--price. allows total control solution process access detailed information guts solver.

title: Interface to the SCIP Optimization Suite
text: Install released version CRAN: install development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("scip") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("bnaras/scip")

title: Interface to the SCIP Optimization Suite

what: scip: R Interface to the SCIP Optimization Suite — scip-package
title: scip: R Interface to the SCIP Optimization Suite — scip-package
text: Provides R interface SCIP (Solving Constraint Integer Programs), framework mixed-integer programming (MIP), mixed-integer nonlinear programming (MINLP), constraint integer programming. Supports linear, quadratic, SOS, indicator, knapsack constraints continuous, binary, integer variables. Includes one-shot solver interface model-building API incremental problem construction.

title: scip: R Interface to the SCIP Optimization Suite — scip-package
text: Maintainer: Balasubramanian Narasimhan naras@stanford.edu (ORCID) contributors: SCIP Optimization Suite Authors (SCIP, SoPlex, PaPILO libraries) [copyright holder]

what: Add an indicator constraint to a SCIP model — scip_add_indicator_cons
title: Add an indicator constraint to a SCIP model — scip_add_indicator_cons
text: binvar = 1 sum(coefs * x[vars]) <= rhs.

title: Add an indicator constraint to a SCIP model — scip_add_indicator_cons
code: scip_add_indicator_cons(model, binvar, vars, coefs, rhs, name = NULL)

title: Add an indicator constraint to a SCIP model — scip_add_indicator_cons
text: model SCIP model. binvar Integer; 1-based index binary indicator variable. vars Integer vector; 1-based variable indices. coefs Numeric vector; coefficients. rhs Numeric; right-hand side. name Character; constraint name.

title: Add an indicator constraint to a SCIP model — scip_add_indicator_cons
text: Integer; 1-based constraint index.

what: Add a linear constraint to a SCIP model — scip_add_linear_cons
title: Add a linear constraint to a SCIP model — scip_add_linear_cons
text: Adds lhs <= sum(coefs * x[vars]) <= rhs.

title: Add a linear constraint to a SCIP model — scip_add_linear_cons
code: scip_add_linear_cons(model, vars, coefs, lhs = -Inf, rhs = Inf, name = NULL)

title: Add a linear constraint to a SCIP model — scip_add_linear_cons
text: model SCIP model. vars Integer vector; 1-based variable indices. coefs Numeric vector; coefficients (length vars). lhs Numeric; left-hand side. Default -Inf. rhs Numeric; right-hand side. Default Inf. name Character; constraint name. Default auto-generated.

title: Add a linear constraint to a SCIP model — scip_add_linear_cons
text: Integer; 1-based constraint index.

what: Add a quadratic constraint to a SCIP model — scip_add_quadratic_cons
title: Add a quadratic constraint to a SCIP model — scip_add_quadratic_cons
text: Adds lhs <= linexpr + quadexpr <= rhs quadexpr = sum(quadcoefs[k] * x[quadvars1[k]] * x[quadvars2[k]]).

title: Add a quadratic constraint to a SCIP model — scip_add_quadratic_cons
code: scip_add_quadratic_cons(   model,   linvars = integer(0),   lincoefs = double(0),   quadvars1 = integer(0),   quadvars2 = integer(0),   quadcoefs = double(0),   lhs = -Inf,   rhs = Inf,   name = NULL )

title: Add a quadratic constraint to a SCIP model — scip_add_quadratic_cons
text: model SCIP model. linvars Integer vector; 1-based variable indices linear part. lincoefs Numeric vector; linear coefficients. quadvars1, quadvars2 Integer vectors; 1-based variable indices quadratic terms. quadcoefs Numeric vector; quadratic coefficients. lhs, rhs Numeric; constraint bounds. name Character; constraint name.

title: Add a quadratic constraint to a SCIP model — scip_add_quadratic_cons
text: Integer; 1-based constraint index.

Default Inf. vtype Character; variable type: "C" (continuous), "B" (binary), "" (integer). Default "C". name Character; variable name. Default auto-generated.

title: Add multiple variables to a SCIP model — scip_add_vars
code: scip_add_vars(model, obj, lb = 0, ub = Inf, vtype = "C", names = NULL)

title: Add multiple variables to a SCIP model — scip_add_vars
text: model SCIP model. obj Numeric vector; objective coefficients. lb Numeric; lower bounds (scalar vector). Default 0. ub Numeric; upper bounds (scalar vector). Default Inf. vtype Character; variable types (scalar vector). Default "C". names Character vector; variable names. Default auto-generated.

title: Add multiple variables to a SCIP model — scip_add_vars
text: Integer; 1-based index first variable added.

title: SCIP solver control parameters — scip_control
text: Create list control parameters SCIP solver. Parameters organized logical groups: output, limits, tolerances, presolving, LP, branching, heuristics. SCIP parameter can also set directly using native path via ....

title: SCIP solver control parameters — scip_control
code: scip_control(   verbose = TRUE,   verbosity_level = 3L,   display_freq = 100L,   time_limit = Inf,   node_limit = -1L,   stall_node_limit = -1L,   sol_limit = -1L,   best_sol_limit = -1L,   mem_limit = Inf,   restart_limit = -1L,   gap_limit = 0,   abs_gap_limit = 0,   feastol = 1e-06,   dualfeastol = 1e-07,   epsilon = 1e-09,   presolving = TRUE,   presolve_rounds = -1L,   lp_threads = 1L,   lp_iteration_limit = -1L,   lp_scaling = TRUE,   branching_score = "p",   heuristics_emphasis = "default",   threads = 1L,   ... )

title: SCIP solver control parameters — scip_control
text: verbose Logical; print solver output. Default TRUE. verbosity_level Integer 0–5; verbosity detail. Default 3. display_freq Integer; node display frequency. Default 100. time_limit Numeric; time limit seconds. Default Inf. node_limit Integer; max nodes. Default -1L. stall_node_limit Integer; stall nodes. Default -1L. sol_limit Integer; solution limit. Default -1L. best_sol_limit Integer; improving solution limit. Default -1L. mem_limit Numeric; memory limit MB. Default Inf. restart_limit Integer; restart limit. Default -1L. gap_limit Numeric; relative MIP gap. Default 0. abs_gap_limit Numeric; absolute gap. Default 0. feastol Numeric; feasibility tolerance. Default 1e-6. dualfeastol Numeric; dual feasibility tolerance. Default 1e-7. epsilon Numeric; zero tolerance. Default 1e-9. presolving Logical; enable presolving. Default TRUE. presolve_rounds Integer; presolve rounds. Default -1L. lp_threads Integer; LP solver threads. Default 1L. lp_iteration_limit Integer; LP iteration limit. Default -1L. lp_scaling Logical; LP scaling. Default TRUE. branching_score Character; score function. Default "p". heuristics_emphasis Character; heuristic emphasis. Default "default". threads Integer; parallel solving threads. Default 1L. See Parallel section caveats. ... Additional SCIP parameters name-value pairs, using SCIP's native hierarchical parameter paths (e.g., "lp/fastmip" = 1, "conflict/enable" = FALSE). See SCIP parameter documentation full list.

title: SCIP solver control parameters — scip_control
text: named list class "scip_control" components: verbose Logical; verbosity flag. scip_params Named list; parameters SCIP native paths.

title: SCIP solver control parameters — scip_control
text: verbose Logical; print solver output. Default TRUE. verbosity_level Integer 0–5; verbosity detail (0 = none, 3 = normal, 5 = full). Default 3. Ignored verbose = FALSE. display_freq Integer; display status line every many nodes (-1 = never). Default 100.

title: SCIP solver control parameters — scip_control
text: time_limit Numeric; maximum solving time seconds. Default Inf (limit). node_limit Integer; maximum number branch--bound nodes. Default -1L (limit). stall_node_limit Integer; nodes without improvement stopping. Default -1L (limit). sol_limit Integer; stop finding many feasible solutions. Default -1L (limit). best_sol_limit Integer; stop many improving solutions. Default -1L (limit). mem_limit Numeric; memory limit MB. Default Inf (limit). restart_limit Integer; maximum restarts. Default -1L (limit).

title: SCIP solver control parameters — scip_control
text: gap_limit Numeric; relative MIP gap tolerance; solver stops gap falls value. Default 0 (prove optimality). abs_gap_limit Numeric; absolute gap primal dual bound. Default 0. feastol Numeric; feasibility tolerance LP constraints. Default 1e-6. dualfeastol Numeric; dual feasibility tolerance. Default 1e-7. epsilon Numeric; absolute values treated zero. Default 1e-9.

title: SCIP solver control parameters — scip_control
text: presolving Logical; enable presolving. Default TRUE. presolve_rounds Integer; maximum presolving rounds (-1 = unlimited). Default -1L.

title: SCIP solver control parameters — scip_control
text: lp_threads Integer; number threads LP solver. Default 1L. lp_iteration_limit Integer; LP iteration limit per solve (-1 = limit). Default -1L. lp_scaling Logical; enable LP scaling. Default TRUE.

title: SCIP solver control parameters — scip_control
text: branching_score Character; branching score function: "s" (sum), "p" (product), "q" (quotient). Default "p".

title: SCIP solver control parameters — scip_control
text: heuristics_emphasis Character; heuristic emphasis setting: "default", "aggressive", "fast", "". Default "default".

title: SCIP solver control parameters — scip_control
text: threads Integer; number threads concurrent solving. Default 1L. Note: concurrent solving may require SCIP build compiled parallel support (e.g., PARASCIP=true); installations provide .

title: SCIP solver control parameters — scip_control
code: ## Quick solve with 60-second time limit ctrl <- scip_control(time_limit = 60)  ## Quiet solve with 1% gap tolerance ctrl <- scip_control(verbose = FALSE, gap_limit = 0.01)  ## Aggressive heuristics, no presolving ctrl <- scip_control(heuristics_emphasis = "aggressive", presolving = FALSE)  ## Pass a native SCIP parameter directly ctrl <- scip_control("conflict/enable" = FALSE, "separating/maxrounds" = 5L)

what: Solve a linear or mixed-integer program using SCIP — scip_solve
title: Solve a linear or mixed-integer program using SCIP — scip_solve
text: One-shot interface SCIP solver. Formulates solves: $$\min_{x} \; obj' x$$ subject constraint rows defined , b, sense, variable types vtype bounds lb, ub.

title: Solve a linear or mixed-integer program using SCIP — scip_solve
code: scip_solve(obj, A, b, sense, vtype = "C", lb = 0, ub = Inf, control = list())

title: Solve a linear or mixed-integer program using SCIP — scip_solve
text: obj Numeric vector length n; objective coefficients. Constraint matrix (m x n). Can dense matrix, dgCMatrix, simple_triplet_matrix. b Numeric vector length m; constraint right-hand side. sense Character vector length m; constraint sense. element must "<=", ">=", "==". vtype Character; variable types. Either single value applied variables, vector length n. Values: "C" (continuous), "B" (binary), "" (integer). Default "C". lb Numeric; lower bounds variables. Single value vector length n. Default 0. ub Numeric; upper bounds variables. Single value vector length n. Default Inf. control list solver parameters, typically scip_control.

title: Solve a linear or mixed-integer program using SCIP — scip_solve
text: named list components: status Character; solver status (e.g., "optimal", "infeasible", "unbounded"). objval Numeric; optimal objective value (NA solution). x Numeric vector; primal solution (NULL solution). sol_count Integer; number solutions found. gap Numeric; relative optimality gap. info List additional solver information (solve_time, iterations, nodes).

title: scip 0.0.2
text: Initial CRAN submission. One-shot solver interface (scip_solve) incremental model-building API (scip_model, scip_add_var, scip_add_linear_cons, scip_add_quadratic_cons, scip_add_sos1_cons, scip_add_sos2_cons, scip_add_indicator_cons). Solver control parameters (scip_control). Sparse matrix support (dgCMatrix, simple_triplet_matrix). Vignette LP, MIP, quadratic, indicator constraint examples. Builds macOS, Linux, Windows using vendored SCIP 10.0.1 SoPlex 8.0.1 sources.
